perturbation.original_text: (e) "Recipe" means the ingredients and methods of combining and processing ingredients for the Products, provided that a Recipe will not include the composition of or recipe for the Neutral Alcohol Beverage Base (it being understood that the amount of Neutral Alcohol Beverage Base and the process for combining and processing it with other ingredients shall be included in the Recipe).
perturbation.changed_text: (e) "Recipe" means only the ingredients and methods of combining and processing ingredients for the Products, provided that a Recipe will not include the composition of or recipe for the Neutral Alcohol Beverage Base (it being understood that the amount of Neutral Alcohol Beverage Base and the process for combining and processing it with other ingredients shall be included in the Recipe) and will not include any intellectual property rights of any kind.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "and will not include any intellectual property rights of any kind" to the definition of "Recipe", we are contradicting section 6(a)(i) which grants Reed's ownership of all Deliverables, which by definition (1(c)) includes the Recipes. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the ownership of Recipes intellectual property rights. This perturbation creates uncertainty about whether Reed's owns the intellectual property rights to the Recipe, even though section 6(a)(i) explicitly assigns those rights to Reed's.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Intellectual Property. (a) Ownership of Intellectual Property. (i) Company will create and provide to Reed's the Deliverables. Reed's will exclusively own all Deliverables. Company will and hereby does, without further consideration, irrevocably assign to Reed's any and all worldwide right, title or interest that Company may now or hereafter possess in or to the Deliverables in perpetuity (or the maximum period permitted by Applicable Laws and Regulations) and Reed's accepts such assignment. Company will execute and deliver documents reasonably requested by Reed's to register its Intellectual Property in the Deliverables.
perturbation.justification: The addition of "and will not include any intellectual property rights of any kind" to the definition of "Recipe" directly contradicts section 6(a)(i). Section 6(a)(i) states "Reed's will exclusively own all Deliverables" and further, "Company will and hereby does... irrevocably assign to Reed's any and all worldwide right, title or interest... in or to the Deliverables". As Deliverables (by definition 1(c)) includes Recipes, the change to the definition of Recipe creates a direct conflict in whether Reed's owns the intellectual property rights. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Company shall be responsible for all costs relating to the development of the Recipes and Deliverables, except for costs and expenses relating to the involvement of Reed's personnel and contractors.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall be responsible for all costs relating to the development of the Recipes and Deliverables, including but not limited to costs and expenses relating to the involvement of Reed's personnel and contractors, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Reed's.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Company is responsible for all costs relating to the development, except for costs involving Reed's personnel. By modifying the text to 'Company shall be responsible for all costs relating to the development of the Recipes and Deliverables, including but not limited to costs and expenses relating to the involvement of Reed's personnel and contractors, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Reed's.' it creates an in-text contradiction and an obligation on Reeds, since Reeds could be responsible for cost relating to Recipe development. This contrasts the original section where Reeds wasn't obliged to contribute to any costs of development.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Consideration. In exchange for Company's contributions and obligations under this Agreement, Reed's grants Company the exclusive right to manufacture, package, promote, sell and distribute the Products (if and to the extent approved by the Development Committee), subject to the terms and conditions of a separate Manufacturing and Distribution Agreement to be entered into by the parties concurrently with this Agreement, as it may be amended, modified, supplemented or restated from time to time (the "Manufacturing and Distribution Agreement"). Company shall be responsible for all costs relating to the development of the Recipes and Deliverables, including but not limited to costs and expenses relating to the involvement of Reed's personnel and contractors, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Reed's.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Company is responsible for all costs except those involving Reed's personnel. The changed text shifts this by making the Company responsible even for costs involving Reed's personnel unless otherwise agreed in writing by Reed's. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. By modifying the agreement in this way, the document now dictates that Company could be responsible for cost relating to Recipe development. This directly contrasts the original section where Reeds wasn't obliged to contribute to any costs of development. This introduces a direct contradiction by changing the allocation of financial responsibility.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that any claim or dispute arising under this Agreement shall be resolved by a court located in New York City, New York.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that any claim or dispute arising under this Agreement may be resolved by a court located in New York City, New York, or through binding arbitration at the discretion of the Reed's.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that all disputes must be resolved in New York City courts. By inserting "or through binding arbitration at the discretion of the Reed's", Reed's now has the unilateral option to force arbitration, removing the Company's right to have the dispute heard in court. This is contradictory because the original agreement was for all disputes to be resolved in New York Court and now there is another alternative which can be chosen at the discretion of Reeds, changing the original obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Governing Law; Venue. This Agreement shall be governed by, and any dispute arising hereunder shall be determined in accordance with, the laws of State of New York (without giving effect to conflict of laws principles) including all matters of construction, validity and performance. The parties agree that any claim or dispute arising under this Agreement may be resolved by a court located in New York City, New York, or through binding arbitration at the discretion of the Reed's.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that disputes 'shall' be resolved in New York City courts, establishing a mandatory venue. The changed text introduces an alternative dispute resolution method ('or through binding arbitration at the discretion of the Reed's'), which contradicts the original mandatory venue by allowing one party to unilaterally choose a different forum. The Contradicted Text and Changed Text are the same. This change contradicts the original obligation that all disputes 'shall be resolved by a court' because it now introduces an alternative at the discretion of one party.
